It should be noted that the container is anchored to the bottom center. ### Steps to reproduce - Run the minimal reproduction project - Press ui_right to show the container and add a button child. summary: Regression causes error by calling ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible() with nullptr

description.text:
### Tested versions

- 4.4.1-stable
- v4.4.2.rc.custom_build [e531f3eb7]
- 4.5b5


### System information

Godot v4.4.2.rc (e531f3eb7) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7680) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-12900H (20 threads)

### Issue description

PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/97878 caused a set of errors, when using a ScrollContainer, possibly as part of an editor plugin. It is reproducible in Terrain3D.  

```
ERROR: Parameter "p_node" is null.
   at: is_ancestor_of (scene/main/node.cpp:2037)
ERROR: Must be an ancestor of the control.
   at: (scene/gui/scroll_container.cpp:293)
ERROR: Error calling deferred method: 'ScrollContainer::ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible': Cannot convert argument 1 from Object to Object.
   at: _call_function (core/object/message_queue.cpp:222)
```

[This is the offending line](https://github.com/mounirtohami/godot/blob/2f87b7aae0d7a5481159fc02eb6bdb83ac3ad1fa/editor/gui/editor_bottom_panel.cpp#L137) that was added. Commenting the line prevents the error. The passed variables in the deferred call are not null at this moment.
```
callable_mp(button_scroll, &ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible).call_deferred(items[p_idx].button);
```

The problem may be in [this function](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/scene/gui/scroll_container.cpp#L326). When the error occurs, this function receives a null for p_control. Because this node is not an ancestor of null, it prints an error. 
```
void ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible(Control *p_control) {
	ERR_FAIL_COND_MSG(!is_ancestor_of(p_control), "Must be an ancestor of the control.");
```

I thought maybe we just need a null check and silent exit of the above function but that leaves this error remaining. Also note that it has `ScrollContainer::` twice:
```
ERROR: Error calling deferred method: 'ScrollContainer::ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible': Cannot convert argument 1 from Object to Object.
   at: CallQueue::_call_function (core\object\message_queue.cpp:222)
```

Looking at all of the Godot source, ensure_control_visible is only called in 3 places. I made this diff to 4.4.2 for some debugging lines:
<details>
  <summary>Debug code</summary>  

```

                                _page_changed(page_index + direction);
                        }

+                       OS::get_singleton()->print("ensure_control_visible editor_properties_array_dict.cpp\n");
                        InspectorDock::get_inspector_singleton()->ensure_control_visible(reorder_slot.container);
                }
        }
diff --git a/editor/gui/editor_bottom_panel.cpp b/editor/gui/editor_bottom_panel.cpp

--- a/editor/gui/editor_bottom_panel.cpp

        switch (p_what) {
@@ -134,6 +136,14 @@ void EditorBottomPanel::_switch_to_item(bool p_visible, int p_idx, bool p_ignore
                if (expand_button->is_pressed()) {
                        EditorNode::get_top_split()->hide();
                }
+               print_line(Time::get_singleton()->get_ticks_usec(), " editor_bottom_panel, idx: ", String::num_int64(p_idx));
+               for (int i = 0; i < items.size(); i++) {
+                       print_line(i, ": ", items[i].name, ", ", items[i].control, ", ", items[i].button);
+                       if (i == 16) {

+                       }
+               }
+               print_line(Time::get_singleton()->get_ticks_usec(), "Setting up ensure_control_visible.call_deferred(items[", p_idx, "].button: ", items[p_idx].button, ")");
                callable_mp(button_scroll, &ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible).call_deferred(items[p_idx].button);
        } else {
                add_theme_style_override(SceneStringName(panel), get_theme_stylebox(SNAME("BottomPanel"), EditorStringName(EditorStyles)));
diff --git a/scene/gui/scroll_container.cpp b/scene/gui/scroll_container.cpp
index fdb134c5d0..f84d69081f 100644
--- a/scene/gui/scroll_container.cpp
+++ b/scene/gui/scroll_container.cpp
@@ -279,6 +279,7 @@ void ScrollContainer::_update_scrollbar_position() {

 void ScrollContainer::_gui_focus_changed(Control *p_control) {
        if (follow_focus && is_ancestor_of(p_control)) {
+               OS::get_singleton()->print("ensure_control_visible ScrollContainer::_gui_focus_changed\n");
                ensure_control_visible(p_control);
        }
        if (draw_focus_border) {
@@ -290,6 +291,9 @@ void ScrollContainer::_gui_focus_changed(Control *p_control) {
 }

 void ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible(Control *p_control) {
+       if (p_control == nullptr) {
+               OS::get_singleton()->print("ensure_control_visible p_control is null\n");
+       }
        ERR_FAIL_COND_MSG(!is_ancestor_of(p_control), "Must be an ancestor of the control.");

        // Just eliminate the rotation of this ScrollContainer.

```
</details>

What I found is that when I do the test operation in Terrain3D that only updates the visibility of children under the ScrollContainer, it calls `EditorBottomPanel::_switch_to_item()` 4 times at least, twice with idx 0, twice with idx 16. 
This dumps `Vector<BottomPanelItem> items;` as `idx: items[i].name, .control, .button` and none of the entries are null leading into the deferred call. 

When the call is received by `ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible(Control *p_control)`, p_control is null, which causes the error message since it's not an ancestor.

[The code that triggers the call](https://github.com/TokisanGames/Terrain3D/blob/main/project/addons/terrain_3d/src/asset_dock.gd#L266-L290) is called when pressing a button on our asset dock, which hides one child of the ScrollContainer and makes another visible (mesh_list and texture_list). Both children are Containers with a bunch of texture buttons in it. I understand ScrollContainer is really only supposed to have one child, however if I change the structure so there's a single Control child, it makes no difference to this error.


### Steps to reproduce

The problem is intermittent so can be flakey, but in build after build after build I've been able to produce it, with new projects, builds by me, builds by github, and countless commits up and down my tree.

* Create a new project
* Change to the asset lib and download Terrain3D and install.
* Enable Terrain3D in the project settings.
* Open Demo.tscn
* Click Terrain3D
* The error occurs when you alternate clicking on Meshes or Textures in the Terrain3D Asset Dock. You may also need to click off Terrain3D in the scene tree, and back on, or change to a new scene and back, then click the buttons.

If you can't get it to trigger with the release build, try our latest artifact. Remove addons/terrain_3d and add it from this file: https://github.com/TokisanGames/Terrain3D/actions/runs/16824689292/artifacts/3717626215

See above.

summary: One-way property of shapes is not respected in TileMapLayer chunks

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: all snapshots between 4.5.dev1 and 4.5.beta5
- Not reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta5 - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 Ti (nvidia; 550.163.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8700K CPU @ 3.70GHz (12 threads) - 15.57 GiB memory

### Issue description

I'm creating 2D platformer and I want to add semisolid tiles that can stack on top of each other.

Since [GH-102662](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/102662) adjacent tiles merge into bigger collision shapes. This process doesn't respect one-way property of shapes and always merge them.
In my case this leads to only topmost tile having one-way collision.
This can be fixed by setting 'physics_quadrant_size' to 1.

physics_quadrant_size = 16

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15f3fa49-877c-4251-ae32-f5cdea2b7db0

physics_quadrant_size = 1

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f494e423-0756-483b-aaed-ce220d50f01c

### Steps to reproduce

MRP:
1. Open project;
2. Run it (Controls: left and right arrows, space for jump);
3. Notice that only topmost tiles have one-way collision.

Manual:
1. Add TileMapLayer to scene;
2. Add tile and assign shape with one-way collision to it;
3. Draw tiles and stack them on top of each other;
4. Add CharacterBody2D with rectangle shape and attach short script for platformer controls:
```GDScript
extends CharacterBody2D

func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void:
	velocity.x = 500.0 * Input.get_axis(&"ui_left", &"ui_right")
	velocity.y += 1000.0 * delta
	
	if is_on_floor() and Input.is_action_just_pressed(&"ui_accept"):
		velocity.y -= 600.0            
	
	move_and_slide()
```
5. Run project and notice that only topmost tiles have one-way collision.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[OneWayTilesBug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21705858/OneWayTilesBug.zip)

summary: Android: Add method to set root window color at runtime
Script Editor hint popups block mousewheel scrolling in script when Editor is set to Single Window Mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproduceable in 4.5 beta5
- Not reproducable in 4.4.1 stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta5 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7700) - AMD Ryzen 7 3700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.94 GiB memory

### Issue description

**EDIT: on further investigation, I found out that it only happens when `interface/editor/single_window_mode` is `true` (Editor set to single window mode)**
When hovering in the script editor above e.g. a function call, you will get the new hint popup.
While the hint popup is shown, mouse wheel scrolling is not working anymore in the script.
This is a major usability impediment as scrolling to a position where the mouse cursor randomly hovers a function call suddenly disables scrolling.

I noticed this is **very annoying** and slowing down my development time.

In the following video I am trying to scroll through a script.
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d420004d-08d0-468c-b156-9399a03efb0b

### Steps to reproduce

open a script (ideally a longer one), scroll through the script slowly - randomly land on a function call with the mouse cursor while scrolling.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

not neccessary, happens in any script when single window mode is enabled.

summary: Crash In Android Editor On Run: Solved by setting the "Game Embed Mode" to "Disabled"

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] (from Google Play Store)

### System information

Android 8 (phone)

### Issue description

Why trying to run the game (from the editor Play button), the editor crashed. Tested on both a friend phone and mine.
I then set the "Game Embed Mode" from "Auto (based on screen size)" to "Disabled". This solved the problem as I can have the project playing.

The game view  is still embedded, which is fine since the top button bar has useful options when working on android like close button... I don't know if this is intentional given that I had to disable the option to have it work.

Proposal/Quick Fix: set this to disabled as default (this does not result in ill behavior on other's device)

### Steps to reproduce

Play the project

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

any

summary: Native File Dialog Implementation for iOS

description.text:
I've been working on a project that needed this support.. A matter of fact it was required.. I couldn't wait anymore to get someone else from the community to do it so I did it myself. Please test thoroughly and make sure no bugs exists..

Anyway.. Enjoy!



## Native File Dialog Implementation

The iOS platform port includes a comprehensive native file dialog implementation using `UIDocumentPickerViewController`, providing seamless integration with iOS's document management system.

### Features

#### Core Functionality
- **Native iOS Integration**: Uses `UIDocumentPickerViewController` for authentic iOS user experience
- **Multiple Dialog Modes**: Supports open file, open multiple files, open directory, and save file operations
- **File Type Filtering**: Advanced filter support with file extensions, MIME types, and UTI (Uniform Type Identifiers)
- **iOS Version Compatibility**: Supports iOS 11+ with optimized features for iOS 14+ using modern `UTType` APIs

#### Security & Permissions
- **Security-Scoped URLs**: Automatic management of iOS security-scoped resource access
- **Sandboxing Compliance**: Full compliance with iOS app sandboxing requirements
- **Permission Validation**: Comprehensive read/write permission checking with fallback mechanisms
- **Safe File Access**: Coordinated file operations using `NSFileCoordinator` for thread-safe access

#### Save Dialog Workflow
- **Temporary File Management**: Intelligent temporary file creation in app's documents directory
- **Unique Filename Generation**: Automatic conflict resolution with incremental naming
- **Export-Based Saving**: Uses iOS document export pattern for user-controlled file placement
- **Content Validation**: Pre-export content verification and size limit checking

#### Advanced Features
- **Multi-File Selection**: Support for selecting multiple files in open modes
- **Directory Selection**: Native folder picker integration for directory-based operations
- **File Size Validation**: Safety checks for large files (>2GB warning system)
- **Path Sanitization**: Automatic filename sanitization for iOS filesystem compatibility
- **UTF-8 Encoding**: Robust Unicode filename support with encoding validation

#### Memory Management
- **Automatic Cleanup**: Smart delegate lifecycle management with timeout protection
- **Resource Deallocation**: Proper cleanup of security-scoped URLs and temporary files
- **Memory Safety**: Strong reference management for async operations

### Technical Implementation

#### Key Components
- **GodotDocumentPickerDelegate**: Custom delegate handling document picker callbacks
- **GodotSecurityScopedURLManager**: Centralized management of security-scoped URL access
- **Temporary File System**: Secure temporary file creation and cleanup in documents directory

#### API Methods
- `file_dialog_show()`: Standard file dialog interface
- `file_dialog_with_options_show()`: Extended dialog with additional options
- `get_temp_file_path_for_save()`: iOS-specific temporary file path generation
- `release_file_access()`: Manual security-scoped URL cleanup
- `release_all_file_access()`: Bulk cleanup of all active file access permissions

#### iOS-Specific Considerations
- **Document Picker Modes**: Automatic mode selection based on dialog type (open/export)
- **UTType Integration**: Modern iOS 14+ type system with legacy fallback support
- **View Controller Management**: Proper presentation from root view controller
- **Text Input Dismissal**: Automatic keyboard dismissal to prevent RTI warnings

### Usage Examples

#### Basic File Opening
```gdscript
DisplayServer.file_dialog_show(
    "Open File",
    "",
    "document.txt",
    false,
    DisplayServer.FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_FILE,
    ["*.txt", "*.pdf"],
    _on_file_selected
)
```

#### Save File with Temporary Workflow
```gdscript
# Step 1: Get temporary file path
var temp_path = DisplayServer.get_temp_file_path_for_save("my_file.txt")

# Step 2: Write content to temporary file
var file = FileAccess.open(temp_path, FileAccess.WRITE)
file.store_string("File content")
file.close()

# Step 3: Show save dialog
DisplayServer.file_dialog_show(
    "Save File",
    "",
    "my_file.txt",
    false,
    DisplayServer.FILE_DIALOG_MODE_SAVE_FILE,
    ["*.txt"],
    _on_file_saved
)
```

### Compatibility & Requirements

- **Minimum iOS Version**: iOS 11.0+
- **Optimized for**: iOS 14.0+ (UTType support)
- **Required Capabilities**: File system access, document picker framework
- **Export Settings**: "Accessible From Files App" recommended for enhanced functionality

This implementation provides a robust, secure, and user-friendly file dialog system that seamlessly integrates with iOS's native document management while maintaining compatibility with Godot's cross-platform file dialog API.

summary: Imported `Image`s are not saved as external resources when used as sub-resources

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: v4.5.beta5.official [c81fd6c51]

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta5 - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti (nvidia; 570.169) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7300HQ CPU @ 2.50GHz (4 threads) - 23.37 GiB memory

### Issue description

Image assets can be imported as different types of resources in the **Import Dock**. Importing as `Image` may be an edge case.

After importing as an `Image`, it may be automatically converted to an `ImageTexture` when dragging and dropping it into the editor's Inspector.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/a3b42d85d27668f8992c0779ed3cc82d13db3dd9/editor/inspector/editor_resource_picker.cpp#L859-L867

However, `ImageTexture::set_image()` does not cache the `Image`. The `resource_path` is lost during data saving and transfer.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/a3b42d85d27668f8992c0779ed3cc82d13db3dd9/scene/resources/image_texture.cpp#L92-L97


https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/a3b42d85d27668f8992c0779ed3cc82d13db3dd9/scene/resources/image_texture.cpp#L127-L133

Each time you get the `Image` from the **same** `ImageTexture`, you may get a **different** newly generated `Image`.

When you save a scene or resource that uses an imported `Image`, the `Image` will embed its data into the saved scene or resource file.

This is expected, but large amounts of non-text data changes can create noise in git.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a project and open it for editing;
2. Duplicate icon.svg as image.svg;
3. Import image.svg as Image in the Import Dock;
4. Create a scene or resource that uses Texture2D and open it;
5. Drag and drop image.svg onto the property that uses Texture2D.
6. Observe that an ImageTexture is automatically generated.
7. Save the scene or resource to a file;
8. Use a text editor to view the contents of the saved file.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[imported-images-not-save-as-external-resources.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21702929/imported-images-not-save-as-external-resources.zip)

summary: When remapping InputMap actions, events get a default device filter of 0 instead of All Devices

description.text:
### Tested versions

Occurs in 4.4 Stable

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

In project settings > InputMap, actions are set by default to listen to device id -1: All Devices. For example, xbox controller button A is set to action "jump". When allowing the player to remap their buttons, an unexpected side effect occurs, where only device 0 works.

Example
1. We erase associated events for an action:
InputMap.action_erase_event(action_name, old_event)
2. Add the replacement event:
InputMap.action_add_event(action_name, new_event)

Expected behavior:
The action should be remapped to the new event, say "jump" is now tied to B instead of A

Actual behavior:
The action is remapped to new_event, exclusively for device 0 and all other devices are ignored

Workaround:
new_event.device = -1
InputMap.action_add_event(action_name, new_event)

Proposed solution:
Make event.device default to -1 instead of 0 to bring it in line with Project Settings behavior for adding controller events

### Steps to reproduce

Even without 2 actual physical xInput controllers it's easy to test:

1. Define a custom inputmap action and associate an event to replace
2. Erase the event
3. Replace the event with your test event

Result: Only device 0 (controller 1) works

The attached MRP demonstrates this with print statements

summary: [Android editor] Theme updates triggers stack overflow when OpenXR is enabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in the Android and XR editor: 4.4.1-stable, 4.5.0-beta5, `master` branch
- Not reproducible on desktop versions of the editor

### System information

- Pixel Pro 9 Fold running Android 16, Pixel 8a (emulator) running Android 15, Quest 3 running HorizonOS

### Issue description

An OpenXR enabled project crashes with a stack overflow error when one of the editor theme properties is updated.

The stack overflow crash originates from: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/a3b42d85d27668f8992c0779ed3cc82d13db3dd9/core/object/object.cpp#L1237-L1246

- When updating the editor theme settings, a signal is emitted to all registered components. 
- With OpenXR enabled in the project, the OpenXR Action map editor and its components are included in the list of registered components, causing the size of the registered components to grow to several thousands
- The logic shown above allocates memory on the stack for all registered components.
- On Android, this runs into the stack limit causing a stack overflow and crashing the editor
  - From my testing, this is not reproducible on MacOs, which I assume is because of the higher stack limit

### Steps to reproduce

- Launch the Android editor
- Use an existing project or create a new project
- Enable OpenXR in the project settings under `XR` -> `OpenXR` -> `Enabled`, and restart the editor as suggested
- Open the editor settings, and navigate to `Interface` -> `Theme`
-  Update one of the theme properties like `Base Color` and confirm
- The editor will crash shortly

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e242227e-36ce-4008-b0d0-b6a95577f697

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

- Any project in the Android / XR editor with OpenXR enabled in the project settings.

summary: Fix imported images not being saved as external resources when used as sub-resources

description.text:
Previously, image assets could be imported as either `Image` or `Texture` types. When imported as an `Image`, dragging and dropping it onto the Texture property in the Inspector would automatically convert it to an `ImageTexture`.

Finally, the `Image` is converted into **pure data** and saved in `TextureStorage`. When trying to fetch the `Image` in the `ImageTexture`, the `Image` is usually newly generated, which loses the `path` property.

When saving the edited scene or the resource being edited in the Inspector, even if the `Image` was imported (an external resource), its data might be embedded in the saved file.

<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

If the image data has not changed, the PR will try to return the source image instead of the newly generated image when call `ImageTexture::get_image()`.  

When saving, it will check whether the resource with `PROPERTY_USAGE_RESOURCE_NOT_PERSISTENT` enabled (mainly `Image`) is an external (imported) resource. If it is an external resource, it will be saved as an external resource. This helps eliminate possible noise in git.

Fix #109479.

summary: Add indicator to linked resources

description.text:
Attempts to somewhat implement https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/904 , https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/9603 , https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10319#issue-2437124230 ## Feature Adds an indicator to tells whether a resource is shared. The button's tooltip shows the number of times a Resource was shared, along with whether there are subresources to be made unique:
![showtime](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4dddada-eb7d-447b-9a23-e032eac21f64) Left Click on the Icon makes Resources Unique. Right Click - Recursive ## Implementation For this feature, after a lot of trial and error, I settled for reduz's approach of using a `HashMap` to track how many times a Resource is referenced by a Node within a Scene. - If a Resource is referenced more than once, you can make it unique (recursively or not). - If there's only one instance of a Resource, it's already unique, so `make_unique` and `make_unique_recursive` is disabled. > [!NOTE] >Duplicating built-in SubResources is also disabled if the 'Parent' Resource wasn't previously made unique. This is because Making a SubResource unique does not matter if the parent isn't also duplicated. See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109458#issuecomment-3190607935 **(gradient example)** - **External** Resources are not added to the `HashMap` — since we can't for certain indicate how many of them there are. Outside from that, I would separate this feature into two parts: ### SceneTree Operations --- In the Scene tree, you can add, delete, duplicate and paste nodes. Each time you do these operations, `update_resource_count` collects built-in non-empty Resources and add them to the HashMap, along with their respective Node. So basically you update the Resource counter each time you do these operations. --- ### Inspector Operations Each time you made a _revelvant_ change to a property that holds a Resource (e.g Made a Resource unique, added a resource to an Array, etc), you update the Resource counter like above, inserting the Resource and its Node in the HashMap. > [!NOTE] >When setting Subresources in the inspector (if not external), they should have the same number of References as the parent. So if Reference `A` is referenced by 3 Nodes, if you add a SubResource `B`, it should also be linked to 3 Nodes. It uses a much "simpler" version of `update_resource_count` (although they could be merged but it would make using this API much more cumbersome for the Inspector and SceneTree). `update_node_reference` collects the Resources found withing a _single_ property and either removes or adds to the HashMap. That way you're only dealing with a specific property is being set and not touching what's already added. In short, The inspector part was the most tricky, as I did have to fix all the edge cases found when making unique, setting or removing Resources . As of 09/28, it's pretty clean all things considered. Using strictly `update_node_reference` and tweaking some pre existing code made it much cleaner than it previously was. ## To-Do List The following is a to-do list of everything that should be dealt with. There are some things left undetermined on how they should work, so I added **Waiting for Input** right after the task. - [x] Display a warning indicator for sub resources as to why they can't be duplicated _(**Waiting on review for input**)_ - [x] ~Move `Make Unique `and `Make Unique recursive ` funcionality to Icons _(**Waiting on review for input**)_~ Edit: NVM, moving functionality to icons hasnt been well supported by users in previous attempts by other PRs - [x] Hashmaps clears and updates itself when Nodes are Added/Removed from a scene - [x] Disable `make_unique` if there's only copy of the Resource or It's a subresource - [x] Update Hashmap through other means, such as using Resource Picker - [x] Counter not updating once you've done an operation. - [x] Track Subresources - [x] Bulk-Setting Node's Resources does not add them to hashmap. - [x] Duplicated Arrays full of Resources do not increase counter for some reason. - [x] External SubResources made unique should be included in the Hashmap, pointing to the parent's Nodes. - [x] Find better way of knowing whether a resource is external or not - [x] ~~History mismatch when undo-redoing packed scenes if they're open on the editor. _**(Waiting on review for input)**_~~ Seems to have fixed itself - [x] Empty Resources/Sub resources using the wrong icon (fixes itself after saving) - [x] Arrays (probly Dictionaries too) don't update counter when making unique - [x] `Inconsistent Redo` error might happen?? - [x] Fix edge case when saving to disk/deleting resource from disk - [x] Dictionaries with Resources as Keys don't show an icon - [x] Edge Case with SubResources not showing the correct amount after adding them to a Resource already referenced by multiple nodes ## Test Project Added a test project so people interested in this feature can test it. It's a pretty dynamic feature though, so please toy around it and report any weird behavior! [indicator-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22394474/indicator-test.zip)

summary: Having a vector font at the end of a bitmap font fallback chain causes the vector font to override all fallbacks.

description.text: ### Tested versions **Reproducible in:** * v4.2.1.stable.mono.official [b09f793f5] * v4.3.stable.mono.official [77dcf97d8] * v4.4.stable.mono.official [4c311cbee] * v4.5.beta5.mono.official [c81fd6c51] ### System information Godot v4.5.beta5.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7680) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-10900K CPU @ 3.70GHz (20 threads) - 31.85 GiB memory ### Issue description When there is a dependency chain of fallback fonts longer than two fonts long (i.e. a falls back to b, which falls back to c), having a vector font in the final point of the dependency chain ("c", in the example order) will cause that vector font to completely override all bitmap fonts from the second point ("b") and beyond. Glyphs defined in the first font ("a") will remain unaffected. ### Steps to reproduce n.b. The MRP contains all of these steps done for you, so don't worry about the setup, only the three test cases. 1. Create three bitmap fonts. * The first should be the letter 'a' in a square texture of any size. Configure the image file to import as a font with its character range only having the letter 'a'. * The second should be the letter 'b' in a square texture of any size. Configure the image file to import as a font with its character range having only the letter 'b'. * The third should contain the letters "ab" in a different visual style (to distinguish them). Configure the image to import as a font with its character range set to 'a'-'b'. 2. Configure the properties of 'a' to have one fallback font: The 'b' bitmap font. 3. Create a new scene with a base type of `Label` (or a `Control` scene with a `Label` child). 4. Configure the font overrides of the label so that its font is 'a'. 5. Enter the string "ab" as its text. The 'a' font should render the first character, and the 'b' font should render the second character, as intended. **Test Case 1:** 1. Configure the properties of 'b' to have one fallback font: The 'ab' bitmap font. 2. Upon reimporting 'b', nothing will change with the `Label` instance, as intended. **Test Case 2:** 1. Configure the properties of 'b' to have one fallback font: A new `SystemFont`. 2. Upon reimporting 'b', the label will display a system 'b' in the label, instead of the contents of the 'b' font. **Test Case 3:** 1. Configure the properties of 'b' to have one fallback font: The 'ab' bitmap font. 2. Reimport 'b' to apply changes. 3. Configure the properties of 'ab' to have one fallback font: A new `SystemFont`. 4. Upon reimporting 'ab', a result similar to Test Case 2 will occur where the system font overrides the letter 'b' in the label's display. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [bm-font-bugtest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21697553/bm-font-bugtest.zip) This test case is for v4.5-beta5. summary: GLTF: Allow controlling how external data is handled on export

description.text: In master, Godot gives you 2 options for exporting, determined only by file type: * Exporting a `.glb` file embeds binary data into a self-contained file (so long as it fits in the 4 GiB limit). * This is equivalent to Blender's "glTF Binary (.glb)" export option. * Exporting a `.gltf` file separates out all data into files, such as images into image files, and buffers into `.bin` files. * This is equivalent to Blender's "glTF Separate (.gltf + .bin + textures)" export option. However, there are more possibilities available than just those two. Blender offers another option called "glTF Embedded (.gltf)" which embeds all data into a text-based `.gltf`. This PR allows doing that with the "Embed Everything" option. Still... there are more possibilities than just those three, so this PR adds a comprehensive enum: The default option is "Automatic", which behaves the same as the current behavior in master. --- * Automatic * When exporting a glTF file, automatically determines if data should be embedded or separated based on the file extension used and if `base_path` is valid. With this option, exporting a `.gltf` file with a valid [member base_path] will behave like "Separate All Files". If exporting a `.glb` file, exporting to an in-memory buffer, or exporting with `base_path` set to an empty string, this will behave like "Embed Everything". * This option is suitable for most use cases. It behaves like Blender's "glTF Binary (.glb)" export option for `.glb` files, and like Blender's "glTF Separate (.gltf + .bin + textures)" export option for `.gltf` files. * Embed Everything * When exporting a glTF file, embeds all data in the glTF file, including images and binary buffers. For `.glb` files, this will use the binary blob chunk at the end of the file to store the data in buffer 0 (if present). For all other buffers, such as buffers in `.gltf` files, the data will be embedded as a base64-encoded string in the JSON of the buffer. * This option is useful when you want a single self-contained file. It behaves like Blender's "glTF Binary (.glb)" export option for `.glb` files, and like Blender's "glTF Embedded (.gltf)" export option for `.gltf` files. * Separate All Files * When exporting a glTF file, separates all files that can be saved in their own file formats into their own files, and also save all raw binary blobs, such as glTF buffers, into separate `.bin` files. * This option is useful when having the glTF file's contents spread out over multiple files is desired, resulting in maximum human readability. It behaves like Blender's "glTF Separate (.gltf + .bin + textures)" export option. * Separate Binary Blobs * When exporting a glTF file, separates all raw binary blobs, such as glTF buffers, into separate `.bin` files. Resources that can be saved in their own file formats, such as images, are embedded within glTF buffers, and are stored in the `.bin` files. * This option is useful if you need efficient storage of binary data while also keeping the number of files low. In the typical case, this option will result in only two files: the glTF file and a single `.bin` file next to it. * Separate Resource Files * When exporting a glTF file, separates all resources that can be saved in their own file formats into their own files, such as images. This option does not separate buffers, meaning that binary blob data without a file type, such as mesh data, animation data, skin/skeleton data, and so on, are still embedded in the glTF file. * This option is useful if the exported glTF file is intended to be added to the project folder in a game engine, so that the game engine can import the resources files separately, and optionally use them separately. Otherwise, game engines may extract embedded resources to their own files, resulting in duplicate data in the project folder. Effectively, this enum serves to answer two questions: * Should resource files, like images, be embedded in the buffers, or separated out into their own files? * Should binary blobs, like buffers, be embedded into the glTF file, or separated out into their own files? Extensions should respect these options by calling `GLTFState.should_separate_resource_files()` to determine if they should save their resources in separate files. --- Test project: [gltf_export_external_data_mode.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21693272/gltf_export_external_data_mode.zip) In the test project, each folder contains the same test cube exported with different choices for the enum. The `glb/automatic/` and `glb/embed_everything/` folders are equivalent, and the `gltf/automatic/` and `gltf/separate_all_files/` folders are equivalent, but are included for completeness.

summary: Fix issues searching `RichTextLabel` when search result is in a table

description.text:
Fixes for cases where search results would be skipped or repeatedly found involving tables in `RichTextLabel`:
- If previous result was found in last cell of table, earlier cells would be skipped, since the end of the table was reached.  Updated to not skip earlier cells when searching in reverse.
- When choosing next line to continue from after searching table, the inner line number within the table's cell was added, causing the search to jump forward if not on line 0 in the cell.  This could cause lines to get skipped when searching forward, or searching the table again when searching in reverse.  Updated to continue from the immediate next line before/after the table.
- If a table cell has multiple lines, repeated searching would only include the line where the previous result was found, then jump to the next cell.  Updated to search remaining lines in the same cell first.

Fixes #103817

Examples of each case below:

Case 1: MRP from #103817.  Can also be reproduced with many class's constructors in the in-editor help.  One example is `AABB`: in the `AABB` documentation, search for "AABB" until you reach the last constructor `AABB AABB(position: Vector3, size: Vector3)`.  When the last AABB is selected, searching previous would jump past the rest of the constructors section.

Case 2: This was the example given in one of the comments in the linked issue.  When searching backward for "bbcode" in the `RichTextLabel` in-editor documentation, the search would get stuck on the "bbcode" result in a codeblock.  Searching for "ready" in the same page would give the same result for codeblocks where "ready" is not on the first line, but would continue searching past the codeblock where "ready" is on the first line.

Case 3: Searching for "install_effect" in the `RichTextLabel` in-editor documentation.  There are two results on different lines in the same code block.  Searching forward would skip over the second result (backward would get stuck due to case 1).

summary: Make `OpenXRCompositionLayer` and its children safe for multithreaded rendering

description.text:
Presently, our implementation of OpenXR composition layers is not thread-safe when used with `rendering/driver/threads/thread_model` set to "Separate".

This can lead to crashes. For example, if you run [this fork of the GDQuest TPS demo](https://github.com/dsnopek/gdquest-tps-demo/tree/standalone-vr-slush-multithreaded) and rapidly press the "Y" button (which will toggle visibility of a composition layer with performance info) it will eventually crash. (NOTE: this is easier to reproduce with the OpenGL renderer, but in order to use that on Quest or other Android-based headsets, you'll need to use PR #109080)

**This PR fixes that crash and makes OpenXR composition layers thread-safe in general!**

I've tested this with a litany of demo projects, covering all types of composition layers including using Android surfaces, on both Vulkan and OpenGL, as well as with both single and multi-threaded rendering. Everything seems to be working fine for me!

I tried really hard to not totally rewrite how we handle OpenXR composition layers, and did a couple of earlier iterations that made smaller changes, but I wasn't able to make it fully thread-safe without going all the way to Godot's "server pattern" and using `OpenXRCompositionLayerExtension` as a proxy to the underlying resources using `RID`s. So, unfortunately, the diff is pretty big and not the easiest to review. However, long-term, I think this is a much cleaner and easier-to-maintain design.

~~**NOTE:** This is currently marked as DRAFT because it builds upon PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108644. I did this, because that PR was already ready to go, and I don't want to cause merge conflicts for the first-time contributor who made it. :-) I'll take this out of DRAFT after that PR is merged~~

summary: `AudioStreamPlayer3D` `volume_db` property mimics attenuation and doesn't actually control volume.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Found in 4.5 while working on other PRs, happens back to 4.0.

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

The `volume_db` property on `AudioStreamPlayer3D` seems to do something along the lines of offsetting/mimicking attenuation, instead of actually adjusting volume. Most notably, it muffles the sound as you lower it. In addition, the point in which the muffling occurs depends on the camera's distance to the stream player. When I am about 2 units away, I can start to hear the muffling when the `volume_db` property is set to -12dB. Between 0dB and -12dB, there is no volume change. When the camera is placed right on top of the AudioStreamPlayer3D (roughly, as in flying the viewport camera over to be on top of it it), muffling becomes noticeable at around -54dB on the slider. Between 0dB and -54dB, there is no muffling/volume change.

`AudioStreamPlayer` and `AudioStreamPlayer2D` seem to work as intended.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create an `AudioStreamPlayer3D`
2. Give it a long sound with pretty even frequency content (such as pink/white noise or a song)
3. With the camera nearby, adjust the `volume_db` property while the sound is playing. Note how there is no volume change. Instead, note how (depending on the distance) it sounds like the sound source becomes attenuated.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[AudioStreamPlayer3D-Volume-Issue-MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21682100/AudioStreamPlayer3D-Volume-Issue-MRP.zip)

I was going to make some fancy UI for testing this when you hit the play button, but honestly it's much easier to just try it out by fiddling with the `volume_db` settings in the inspector after enabling `playing` on the `AudioStreamPlayer3D`. I've also included an `AudioStreamPlayer` and `AudioStreamPlayer2D` to see what the behavior should be like.

Open the file in the File System dock to inspect the cause. ### System information Windows10- both Godot 4.4 stable and Godot 4.5-beta5_mono_win64 - GPU RTX3060 ### Issue description GSGL shader parser can not parse multiple line comment syntax , if "/*" and "*/" are in different line, which makes the import of external gsgl libraries very difficult. ### Steps to reproduce 1.create a glsl file or use any bug-free glsl file in any Godot project 2. type glsl multiple line comment syntax "/**/"and make sure "/*" and "*/" are in different line. 3.save the file and go back to the Godot window. the error message will pop out. (error message was translated by AI) Error occurred while importing the GLSL shader file: "res://glsltest.glsl". Open the file in the File System dock to inspect the cause. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: Shortcut keys not functioning in the SpriteFrames Animation Player

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in v4.5.beta5.official [c81fd6c51] and v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information Godot v4.5.beta5 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7688) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900HX (32 threads) - 63.74 GiB memory ### Issue description When the SpriteFrames panel for AnimatedSprite2D is in focus, the shortcut keys described in the icons don't function at all. All of these commands only work for me if I click the buttons manually. So hitting A, SHIFT + A, S, SHIFT + D, and D doesn't process any of those things. I have to manually click the buttons. Tried it on 4.4.1 and 4.5 and had the same problem. I also checked the command palette and there are no shortcuts to bind in there. Hitting CTRL + N, WILL make a new animation properly. ### Expected Behavior I expect that pressing D on the keyboard will cause the animation to start playing from the current position, ect. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Select the AnimatedSprite2D Node under the Player node in the Player scene. 2. click into the Animation player 3. Press any of the shortcut keys (A, SHIFT + A, S, SHIFT + D, and D) ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [SpriteFrames Bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21674887/SpriteFrames.Bug.zip)

summary: AtlasTexture not working with StandardMaterial3D

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in 4.4.1-stable and 4.5-beta5

### System information

Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 745 - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4790 CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads) - 7.95 GiB memory

### Issue description

I have a MeshInstance3D with a PlaneMesh. I need to get a section of an image onto the mesh, so I tried using a AtlasTexture in a StandardMaterial3D, but it doesn't display anything. I reloaded the project and now it displays the whole image on the mesh, but not a section, just the whole thing.

### Steps to reproduce

_(Open the MRP and see the README or follow these steps in a new project)_
1. Create a MeshInstance3D node
2. Add a PlaneMesh or QuadMesh (I used a PlaneMesh)
3. Add a StandardMaterial3D to the material property
4. Add an AtlasTexture to the albedo_texture property
5. Add an image to the atlas property of the AtlasTexture
6. Select the region (don't make it the whole image)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[AtlasTextureBug-MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21674077/AtlasTextureBug-MRP.zip)
_See README in the above file_

summary: When progress bar y size is less than 27, the _resized signal is called.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4

### System information

4.4

### Issue description

When progress bar y size is less than 27, the _resized signal is called.

### Steps to reproduce

Set your progress bar y size to 26 or lower, then print 'yes' on the _resized signal. It will activate even though it shouldn't.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[resize_bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21673825/resize_bug.zip)
